evals.prompt: Use the doca-rdma skill to help me. I'm working on: doing hands-on DOCA RDMA programming on a BlueField DPU, ConnectX NIC, or DOCA host — bringing up an RDMA context on a doca_dev, picking a connection method (RDMA CM, bridge/OOB, or gRPC exchange of doca_rdma_export()), enabling one of the eleven task types (Send/Receive/Send-Imm, Read/Write/Write-Imm, Atomic CmpSwap/FetchAdd, Get/Set/Add Remote Sync Event), setting matching mmap + RDMA permissions, sizing queues and connections, querying doca_rdma_cap_*, or debugging DOCA_ERROR_* from an RDMA call.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the doca-rdma skill and answers from its documented workflow, quoting only real symbols/commands from the bundle (no invented tokens).
assertions: The doca-rdma skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
Every symbol, flag, or command named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "one-sided read returns permission denied". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-rdma issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-rdma skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "completions never arrive after submit". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-rdma issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-rdma skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I actually need help with general RDMA / ibverbs theory. Which skill should handle this, and why is it not doca-rdma?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-rdma; it explains that general RDMA / ibverbs theory is out of doca-rdma's scope and routes to the appropriate skill instead.
assertions: doca-rdma is not selected as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s) instead.
